They are not for human or veterinary use, food, cosmetic application or in vivo diagnostic procedures. Buyer assumes all responsibility for appropriate use and compliance with applicable regulations.

home.metaTitle: Research peptides · Lab-verified, COA included | Peptide Shop
home.metaDescription: Research-grade peptides for laboratories. Batch HPLC testing, COA on every product, discreet dispatch and clear research-use-only compliance.
home.heroLead: Every batch is HPLC-tested and dispatched with a lot-specific certificate of analysis. Built for laboratories that judge a supplier by their documentation, not their packaging.
home.qualityItem1Body: HPLC trace, MS where assigned, identity & purity statement.
home.qualityItem2Body: Discreet fulfilment with handling notes per product class.
home.qualityItem3Body: Research-use only — never marketed as therapy or supplement.
home.trustCoaLanguages: EN · DE · NL · FR · IT · ES CoAs
home.whyTitle: Built for laboratories that read the certificate first.
home.whyLead: Most catalogues bury the analytical evidence. We surface it on the product page, on the CoA, and in the documentation flow — so procurement and bench scientists never have to chase data.
home.value1Body: Each lot is tested on validated reverse-phase methods. The analytical trace is part of the CoA you receive at dispatch.
home.value2Body: Lot-specific CoAs are downloadable in EN, DE, FR, ES — both as static HTML and dynamically rendered PDF.
home.value3Body: Stocked, packed and shipped with handling guidance per product class. No trans-shipment, no surprise delays.
home.value4Body: Every product page, listing and CoA carries a research-use-only frame. We do not market or imply therapeutic use.
home.researchAreasDescription: Hub-and-spoke navigation organised around the science, not lifestyle categories.
home.useCasesLead: Use-case hubs aggregate compounds across research areas — useful when the experimental design crosses multiple biological systems.
home.featuredDescription: Compact product cards link to full specifications, CoA downloads and ordering.
home.statCoaLanguages: CoA languages — EN · DE · NL · FR · IT · ES
home.insightsDescription: Practical notes on storage, handling, CoA review and reproducibility.
home.ctaTitle: Open the catalogue, or speak to the supply team.
home.ctaBody: Browse {count} catalogued peptides with verifiable documentation, or get in touch about bespoke COA requirements or larger procurement.

about.metaDescription: Peptide Shop supplies research-use peptides with lot documentation, analytical verification and a quality-first dispatch workflow.
about.title: Research materials, built around the documentation.
about.lead: Peptide Shop serves laboratories and qualified researchers who judge a supplier by the certificates that follow each lot — not by the colour of the box it ships in.
about.whatBody1: We supply catalogue research peptides and related laboratory materials for in-vitro research workflows. The site is structured around research areas, use-case hubs and product pages so buyers can move from biological context to compound specifications quickly.
about.whatBody2: Every product page connects upward to the area it belongs to and downward to the certificate of analysis you can preview or download in one click — in English, German, French or Spanish.
about.figureAlt: Organised research peptide vials in a protective case with syringes
about.quote: “Documentation should travel with the molecule, not chase it.”
about.principlesTitle: Four standards we don't bend on.
about.principle1Body: Product pages surface purity, molecular weight, CAS data, storage conditions and CoA links — never hidden behind a sales contact form.
about.principle2Body: Products are labelled for laboratory research only. We never market or imply medicinal, supplemental or cosmetic use.
about.principle3Body: Each product connects to research-area and use-case hubs, reducing reliance on keyword-heavy category pages.
about.principle4Body: Researchers can request CoAs, handling guidance and supply details before committing to larger procurement orders.
about.timelineLead: A simple, auditable path. Each step produces an artefact you can verify on the certificate that ships with your order.
about.timeline1Body: Material is sourced from validated manufacturing partners and re-tested in-house for identity and purity.
about.timeline2Body: HPLC purity is confirmed against the product specification. Mass spectrometry confirms identity where assigned.
about.timeline3Body: A lot-specific certificate is generated and indexed. Available immediately as HTML or PDF in EN, DE, FR, ES.
about.timeline4Body: Cold-chain handled where required. Paperwork travels with the parcel; digital copies are linked from the product page.
about.ctaTitle: All products are supplied for laboratory research and development.
about.ctaBody: They are not intended for human use, veterinary use, therapy, diagnosis, cosmetic use or consumption.

quality.metaDescription: Learn how Peptide Shop presents purity, identity, storage and COA documentation for research peptides.
quality.title: Quality data that supports reproducible research
quality.description: Research outcomes depend on reagent identity, purity and handling. Our quality pages explain the documentation and controls expected for peptide procurement.
quality.hplcBody: High-performance liquid chromatography is used to quantify peptide purity and show the separation profile for batch documentation.
quality.massBody: Mass spectrometry supports molecular identity by comparing observed mass with the theoretical peptide mass.
quality.traceBody: COA references and product IDs make it easier to connect received material with internal procurement records.
quality.storageBody: Storage guidance is shown on product pages so labs can plan receipt, refrigeration and aliquoting workflows.
quality.certificateBody: COAs may include batch number, date of analysis, purity method, molecular identity confirmation, appearance and storage information. Availability can vary by item and lot, so researchers should request current documentation when needed for institutional records.
quality.handlingBody: Lyophilised peptides should be kept dry, protected from light and stored according to the product specification. Reconstituted materials should follow validated laboratory SOPs, including solvent choice, final concentration, aliquoting and disposal.

contact.metaTitle: Contact Peptide Shop | COA and Research Supply Support
contact.metaDescription: Contact Peptide Shop for COA requests, research supply questions and order support.
contact.title: Talk to the research-supply team.
contact.lead: CoA requests, product specifications, dispatch questions or institutional procurement — send the request with a few details and we'll route it to the right person.
contact.name: Name
contact.messagePlaceholder: Include product name, batch reference or order number where relevant.
contact.replyHelp: We typically reply within one business day.

support.coaMetaDescription: Certificate of Analysis policy for Peptide Shop research materials, including HPLC, MS and batch documentation expectations.
support.coaDescription: COAs help laboratories document peptide identity, purity and lot traceability before material enters a research workflow.
support.coaIncludeProduct: Product name, product ID and batch or lot reference.
support.coaIncludeHplc: HPLC purity result and chromatographic method summary where available.
support.coaIncludeMass: Mass spectrometry identity confirmation where available.
support.coaIncludeStorage: Appearance, storage recommendation and date of analysis.
support.coaRequestBody: Use the contact page with the product name and batch reference if you need current COA documentation for institutional review. Where a product page links a COA, researchers should still verify that the document matches the received lot.
support.coaScopeBody: COA data supports laboratory quality records. It does not convert a research material into a medicine, diagnostic, cosmetic, food or supplement, and it is not a substitute for local validation of assay performance.
support.shippingMetaDescription: Shipping, handling and dispatch information for Peptide Shop research materials across the EU and selected international destinations.
support.shippingDescription: Research materials should arrive with packaging, documentation and timing that fit laboratory receiving procedures.
support.dispatchBody: Orders are prepared after payment and compliance review. Dispatch timing can vary by product availability, order size and documentation requirements.
support.temperatureBody: Most lyophilised peptide materials are stable for normal courier transit when sealed and dry. Temperature-sensitive products or support materials may require different handling instructions, which should be checked on the product page or COA.
support.receivingInspect: Inspect packaging before accepting material into storage.
support.receivingMatch: Match product labels against order records and COA references.
support.receivingStore: Move products to the stated storage condition promptly.
support.receivingRecord: Record any damage or discrepancy and contact support with order details.
support.internationalBody: Researchers ordering from outside the EU are responsible for checking import permissions, customs requirements and institutional procurement rules before ordering.
support.needDocumentsBody: Include your order reference, product name and batch number when asking about shipment or COA records.
support.faqMetaDescription: Answers to common questions about Peptide Shop research-use products, COAs, storage, shipping and ordering.
support.faqDescription: Quick answers for research-use purchasing, documentation, handling and site navigation.
support.faqHumanUseQ: Are Peptide Shop products for human use?
support.faqHumanUseA: No. Products are supplied for laboratory research only and are not medicines, foods, supplements, cosmetics or diagnostics.
support.faqResearchAreaQ: Where should I start if I know the research area?
support.faqResearchAreaA: Use the research catalogue to browse by biological area such as diabetes, neuroscience, cell signalling or tissue repair.
support.faqCoaA: COA availability is shown or requested by product and lot. A COA may include HPLC purity, identity confirmation and batch details.
support.faqHplcA: HPLC purity describes the proportion of the target compound detected under a defined chromatographic method. It does not replace local assay validation.
support.faqStorageQ: How should lyophilised peptides be stored?
support.faqStorageA: Follow the storage condition on the product page and COA. Many lyophilised peptides are stored frozen, sealed, dry and protected from light.
support.faqMinimumQ: Is there a minimum order value?
support.faqMinimumA: Yes. A minimum order value of €150 applies at checkout.
support.stillNeedHelpBody: For specific batch, COA or dispatch questions, include product and order details in your message.

legal.termsMetaDescription: Terms governing research-use purchases, buyer responsibilities, ordering, shipping and permitted use of Peptide Shop materials.
legal.termsDescription: These terms set expectations for research-use procurement, permitted use, documentation and buyer responsibility.
legal.termsRestrictionBody: Products are supplied solely for laboratory research and development. They must not be used in humans, animals, foods, cosmetics, supplements, diagnostics or therapeutic applications.
legal.termsBuyerAble: Buyers must be legally able to purchase and handle research chemicals.
legal.termsBuyerLaws: Buyers are responsible for local laws, institutional approvals and safety procedures.
legal.termsBuyerHandle: Buyers must store, handle and dispose of products according to applicable requirements.
legal.termsOrdersBody: Orders may be reviewed, delayed or cancelled where compliance, stock or documentation requirements cannot be met. Pricing, package sizes and availability may change without notice.
legal.termsDocumentationBody: COAs and product data are provided to support research records. They do not represent approval for clinical, diagnostic or consumer use.
legal.privacyMetaDescription: Privacy policy explaining how Peptide Shop handles enquiries, orders, account data and compliance-related records.
legal.privacyDescription: We collect only the information needed to operate the site, respond to enquiries, process orders and maintain lawful research-use records.
legal.privacyCollectBody: We may collect contact details, order information, account details, delivery information, payment status, compliance correspondence and technical site usage data.
legal.privacyUseEnquiries: To respond to enquiries and support requests.
legal.privacyUseOrders: To process orders and arrange dispatch.
legal.privacyUseDocuments: To provide COAs, invoices and product documentation.
legal.privacyUseCompliance: To meet legal, tax, fraud-prevention and research-chemical compliance duties.
legal.privacyRetentionBody: Records are retained for as long as needed for operational, legal and compliance purposes. Information may be shared with service providers such as payment, fulfilment, hosting or professional advisers where required.
legal.privacyChoicesBody: Contact us to request access, correction or deletion where applicable. Some records may need to be retained for legal or compliance reasons.
legal.privacyContactBody: Use the contact page for privacy requests and include the email address associated with the enquiry or order.
legal.disclaimerMetaDescription: Research-use-only disclaimer for Peptide Shop peptides and laboratory materials.
legal.disclaimerDescription: Peptide Shop materials are supplied for qualified laboratory research workflows and are not consumer products.
legal.disclaimerBannerHeading: Not for human or veterinary use.
legal.disclaimerBannerDetail: Products are not medicines, foods, supplements, cosmetics or diagnostics.
legal.disclaimerPermittedBody: Products may be used only in lawful laboratory research and development settings by qualified individuals or organisations with appropriate facilities, procedures and approvals.
legal.disclaimerNoDiagnostic: No diagnostic, therapeutic, cosmetic, food or supplement use.
legal.disclaimerNoResale: No resale or relabelling for consumer purposes.
legal.disclaimerScientificBody: Educational and product content is provided to support research procurement. It is not medical advice and should not be interpreted as dosage, treatment or safety guidance for humans or animals.
legal.disclaimerBeforeBody: Confirm that your intended use is lawful, institutional approvals are in place and your lab has suitable storage and handling procedures.

bundles.metaDescription: Research bundle guidance for selecting related peptide materials by area, workflow and documentation needs.
bundles.title: Research bundle guidance
bundles.description: Bundles should group related research materials by assay context, documentation needs and storage workflow rather than broad consumer claims.
bundles.metabolicBody: Compare GLP-1, GIP, glucagon and amylin pathway materials for diabetes and metabolism research.
bundles.repairBody: Evaluate BPC-157, TB-500 and GHK-Cu across cell migration, ECM and repair models.
bundles.cnsBody: Group Semax, Selank and DSIP for neuropeptide, stress-response and sleep-related workflows.
bundles.handlingBody: Pair lyophilised materials with appropriate storage, reconstitution and COA review processes.
bundles.buildBody: Start from a research-area hub, select products with comparable assay relevance, then confirm package size, storage and COA availability for each item. For larger planned studies, contact the team before ordering so documentation expectations are clear.

learn.metaTitle: Learn About Research Peptides | Peptide Shop

learn.title: Research peptide education
learn.description: Plain-language guides for laboratory teams working with peptide nomenclature, storage, purity, and research-use documentation.
learn.catalogueBody: Browse products by research area once you are familiar with storage and RUO requirements.

peptides.title: What are peptides?
peptides.description: A research-focused introduction to peptides, amino acid chains, synthesis, purity, and storage considerations.

learnArticle.metaTitle: What Are Peptides? Research Peptide Guide | Peptide Shop
learnArticle.metaDescription: Learn what peptides are, how synthetic peptides are made, and how HPLC purity, COA documentation, and storage apply to research use.
learnArticle.title: What are peptides?
learnArticle.lead: Peptides are short chains of amino acids used throughout biology and laboratory research. Their sequence, purity, handling, and storage all affect how reliably they can be used in experiments.
learnArticle.briefBody1: A peptide is a chain of amino acids joined by peptide bonds. Short sequences may act as signalling molecules, receptor ligands, enzyme substrates, or model compounds in laboratory studies. Larger chains with complex folded structures are generally classified as proteins.
learnArticle.briefBody2: Research peptide nomenclature often includes the sequence, molecular weight, CAS number where assigned, purity method, and storage requirements. Those details help researchers compare materials and reproduce experimental conditions.
learnArticle.synthesisBody: Synthetic peptides are commonly manufactured by solid-phase peptide synthesis. Amino acids are added sequentially to a resin-bound chain, then the peptide is cleaved, purified, and analysed using methods such as HPLC and mass spectrometry.
learnArticle.ruoDetail: Analytical documentation supports laboratory identification and purity checks. It does not make a product suitable for human or veterinary use.
learnArticle.purityBody1: HPLC purity indicates the estimated proportion of the target peptide under a stated chromatography method. A COA may also include batch identity, appearance, mass confirmation, water content, or other lot-specific observations.
learnArticle.purityBody2: Lyophilised peptides are typically stored sealed, dry, and frozen. Reconstituted solutions are more vulnerable to hydrolysis, microbial contamination, and freeze-thaw stress, so researchers should follow validated laboratory protocols and the batch COA.
learnArticle.qualityDocumentationBody: Review how COA information is handled before placing a research supply order.

glp1.name: GLP-1 agonists
glp1.description: Incretin-pathway peptides used in metabolic and glucose-homeostasis research.

gh.name: Growth hormone secretagogues
gh.description: Compounds studied for growth-hormone release and endocrine signalling pathways.

repair.name: Tissue repair peptides
repair.description: Sequences investigated in cell migration, tissue repair, and extracellular matrix models.

neuro.name: Neuropeptides
neuro.description: Peptides used in cognitive, stress-response, and neuroprotective research models.

difference.question: What is the difference between peptides and proteins?
difference.answer: Peptides are shorter chains of amino acids, commonly described as 2-50 amino acids, while proteins are larger chains with more complex folded structures.

synthesis.question: How are synthetic peptides made?
synthesis.answer: Most synthetic research peptides are produced by solid-phase peptide synthesis, where amino acids are added step by step to a resin-bound chain before cleavage, purification, and analytical testing.

hplc.question: What does HPLC purity mean?
hplc.answer: HPLC purity is an analytical estimate of how much of a sample corresponds to the target peptide peak under the stated chromatography method.

pharma.question: Are research peptides pharmaceutical products?
pharma.answer: No. Research peptides are supplied for laboratory research use only and are not intended for human or veterinary diagnostic, therapeutic, cosmetic, or consumption purposes.

storage.question: How should lyophilised peptides be stored?
storage.answer: Lyophilised peptides are typically stored sealed, dry, and frozen. Reconstituted material should be handled according to validated laboratory protocols and the supplier COA.

ageGate.title: Are you 18 or older?
ageGate.body: Our products are research chemicals supplied for in-vitro laboratory use only. By entering you confirm you are at least 18 years of age and accept our Terms.
ageGate.legalNote: Per § 9 Jugendschutzgesetz (Germany), access to this site is restricted to adults.
